feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Business One
feed.subtitle: SAP Business One Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: How to restrict users from accessing B1_SHF Attachment
entry.content:
Need your suggestions/guidance for:

How to restrict users from accessing B1_SHF and Attachment folder on server. Any user can access the shared files and copy or remove/delete the files from there. This will be security breach to any business. We want to restrict such folders to users where they should not remove anything, and it should not affect any SAP functions.

Version: 10.0 FP 2402 HF1

entry.title: Non-Deductible VAT Code and Freight in Purchase Invoice
entry.content:
Good morning everyone,

When recording a purchase invoice using a non-deductible tax code and adding freight in the foot of the document, in the resulting accounting entry the non-deductible part is not added to the cost of the freight but to the cost of the invoice line item (either using item codes or the service type document).

Thanks in advance for your help,

Federico

entry.title: Find out the last pricing details for the Child Items of BOM or Individual Item
entry.content:
We are creating the RD Code when item is under RnD Level. And after complete the RnD, New SAP Code is created for Production purpose.

Now user require the last pricing details of RnD item against new SAP Code. So I create UDF filed as "U_Ref_RD_No", where User entered the Old RD item against New Code.

Hence following query is written and it works but only for Assembly item. I want to add the condition that whether Old R&D Item is individual item then it shows that only items pricing details or if it is a assembly then it shows the pricing details of the all child items.

SELECT T2.[Itemcode]as 'New Code',T0.[Code] as 'Old RD Ref Assy Code',
T0.[TreeType],
T1.[Code], T1.[ItemName], T1.[Quantity], T1.[Price]as 'Unit Price','Total Price'= T1.[Quantity]*T1.[Price]

FROM OITT T0 INNER JOIN ITT1 T1 ON T0.[Code] = T1.[Father]
INNER JOIN OITM T2 ON T0.[Code] = T2.[U_Ref_RD_No]

WHERE T2.itemcode ='[%0]'

entry.title: Can not login to SAP Service Layer. Please Help
entry.content:
Hello

When I POST to https://SAP-BO-SERVER:50000/b1s/v1/Login

{
    "CompanyDB":"VCC",
    "UserName":"manager",
    "Password":"1111"
}
SAP Service Layer return error
{
    "error": {
        "code": 100000027,
        "message": {
            "lang": "en-us",
            "value": "Error while connecting to database server SAP-BO-SERVER\n"
        }
    }
}
Please help me fix it.
Thank you very much. Sorry for my English

entry.title: Pick list date must be between order date and cancellation date Message[173-89] SAP B1
entry.content:
Dear all,

When creating a pick list then SAP is showing message like 

Pick list date must be between order date and cancellation date Message[173-89]

please do the needful on that.

 

Regards,

Nikunj

entry.title: Query for which sales order lines has the discount or price been adjusted
entry.content:
Hi,

We are a company with a total of 10 branches. All branches work with sales orders. The employees at the branches have total control over sales orders. They can manually adjust discounts and prices on the customer order. This is also necessary and I therefore do not want to block it.

However, I would like to check the order lines that have been manually adjusted. This mainly concerns the price and the discount percentage. So I am looking for a query that shows me at line level which prices and discounts have been adjusted compared to the standard data entered in price lists, discount groups and special prices for business partners.

For example, an employee checks out a bag of apples. SAP automatically fills in the 20% discount percentage entered for item groups as standard. The employee adjusts the discount percentage to 30%.

Is something so simple to do? Or is this going to be a complicated query?

Thanks

entry.title: Renewal of SAP Business One
entry.content: We stopped using SAP Business One from Dec-2023. We did not pay AMC for our 33 licenses. Now in July-2024, we want to restart SAP. We are ready to pay AMC and penalty for delay. What will be the status of 33 licenses now ? Can we get all the 33 licenses ?

entry.title: Create A/R Invoice + Payment using Service Layer
entry.content:
Dear experts,

Please advise how to create the "A/R Invoice + Payment" type document in SAP B1 using the Service Layer. I can successfully create an A/R Invoice separately and Payment separately, but how do I post the A/R Invoice + Payment (in one SL call or multiple) so that the document appears in the "A/R Invoice + Payment" list in SAP B1 ?

Inspecting and comparing the fields I get when reading both the A/R Inv + Payment and just the A/R Inv documents (both using the Invoices service, created manually in the client) I can only idenfity the difference being the InvoicePayment = tYes and InvoicePayment = tNo respectively. Creating Invoice and Payment separately and then PATCHing the Invoice to set the InvoicePayment = tYes does not seem to accomplish the task, the InvoicePayment remains tNo for me.

entry.title: Stamp Duty Tax Configuration in SAP Business One for Morocco in France Localization
entry.content:
Scenario

As per Moroccan government norms, each sales invoice has a 0.25% stamp duty tax for all cash customers. Importantly, the tax should be calculated at the document level, not at the row or item level.

Currently, we have experience configuring tax codes at the row or item level according to government norms but are not aware of how to configure tax at the document level. It seems it might not be possible directly.

Assistance Needed

Please help us sort out this issue at the earliest. Specifically, we need guidance on:

  1. How to configure the stamp duty tax at the document level.
  2. Any workaround will be there if its not available in standard SAP B1

entry.title: Delivery note to Pick List link query SAP Business One HANA
entry.content:
Dear all,

I need a query which connect the delivery note to relevant pick list, SAP Business One HANA.

Regards,

Nikunj Mehta

entry.title: Is SAP Business One version 10.0 compatible with Windows 7 ?
entry.content:
Is SAP Business One version 10.0 compatible with Windows 7 ?

I did install SAP B1 10.0 on Windows 7, it worked fine excepted the Crystal Reports where I had an error smi18n.dll missing.

The solution was found on the KBA note
https://me.sap.com/notes/0003378700
and
https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/error-upon-preview-cr-layout-the-program-can-t-start-because-smi18n-dll-is/qaq-p/12586547

I cut the C:\Program Files (x86)\SAP BusinessObjects\Crystal Reports for .NET Framework 4.0\Common\SAP BusinessObjects Enterprise XI 4.0\win64_x64, cut the file secSSO.dll

and now all seems to work fine

entry.title: SAP Business One MT messages
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

As you aware MT messages are no longer be supported by SWIFT.
We would like to know in future B1 will also support MX format or it will have some internal conversion?

Thank you.

entry.title: Batch operator script engine service layer sap b1
entry.content:
In service layer SAP b1 have support batch with b1s/v1/$batch. but I want implement it in script engine SAP B1.

please help me and suggestions any idea for this case. 
@Trinidad @ANKIT_CHAUHAN  please help it.

thanks you

entry.title: How to cancel / reverse the picked quantity from the pick list SAP Business one
entry.content:
Dear all,

How to cancel / reverse the picked quantity from the pick list SAP Business one?  

Or suggest the other workaround for same.

 

kind regards,

Nikunj

entry.title: SAP B1 for HANA Email Alerts showing a common Subject
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

Does anyone know what settings need to be done, to change the standard subject line which is seen on all email alerts sent from SAP B1, as below:

Subject: SAP Business One mail message, the subject is in the message body   

 
These settings changed after scheduling reports from the Analytical Portal. Before that, the email subjects were the same as the alert subject.

entry.title: Updating Approved Documents not going for approval
entry.content:
We have noticed, that our PO entered via the Approval process can be updated by the originator or any other user and saved without the document going back for approval.
We have enabled the below option since we want the user to update the document if needed, but the document should be returned for approval.
If we disable the enable updating option, the user is not able to update the document at all. This is not our requirement.
In the earlier version of SAP, an updated document would be sent back for approval was working.
Could you please explain this behaviour from SAP B1 10?

entry.title: Authorization Issue on Connecting Addon getting Error For Two users, others are Working Fine
entry.content:
While connecting Addon getting error for particular users which were working for other users.

Working when full authorization given on UDT and UDO registrations under Customization, but working for others users with No authorization.

Tried copying authorization of working users, still same issue.

Attached error screenshot for reference.

SAP Business One

entry.title: Approval procedure using Query for 2 conditions UDF and Price
entry.content:
Dear Team,

We need to trigger approval for PO based on header Level UDF and Price combination we are using below query and its throwing error

Query:
SELECT Distinct 'TRUE' FROM OPOR where $[OPOR."U_EA_Item"]='Y' and $[OPOR."DocTotal"]<10000

Error:

Could not commit transaction: Error -1 detected during transaction

Please provide solution for this we need to have a approval procedure on 2 conditions at header level.

entry.title: Problem when opening add-on form in SAPB1.
entry.content:
When initializing the add-on, it creates new options in the modules menu, when clicking on them to deploy the form it does not appear, this has been happening to me repeatedly in SAP 10 FP2111 with HANA version.

The code I use is the following:

Public Sub LoadFormXML(ByVal FileName As String, ByRef SBO_Application As SAPbouiCOM.Application)

Dim oXmlDoc As Xml.XmlDocument
oXmlDoc = New Xml.XmlDocument
Dim sPath As String
sPath = IO.Directory.GetParent(System.Windows.Forms.Application.StartupPath).ToString
sPath = System.Environment.CurrentDirectory
oXmlDoc.Load(sPath & "\" & FileName)
SBO_Application.LoadBatchActions(oXmlDoc.InnerXml)

End Sub


It does not seem to give me problems in other SAP environments.
The error is solved automatically when opening any SAP window. Even if you leave the system log messages active, just by being active the error does not happen, but the correct thing is that no extra action is needed by SAP and the .xml forms are displayed without problem.

Has this happened to anyone and does anyone have a workaround for this?

entry.title: ApprovalRequest - Substitute Approval Using Service Layer
entry.content:
How should we send an object in the SAP Business One service layer to execute document approval where the approver is the substitute approver? This means that when the principal approver is on vacation, the substitute can carry out the approval. Currently, in SAP Business One, to perform an approval, we send the following object:
    "ApprovalRequestDecisions": [
        {
            "ApproverUserName": "Approver",
            "ApproverPassword": "ApproverPassword",
            "Status": "ardApproved"
        }
    ]
